nodes.name: ws_listener.py
nodes.summary: Async Python daemon that connects to a VideoDB WebSocket, persists events to a JSONL file, manages a PID file, and reconnects with exponential back-off on transient errors.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses asyncio with structured concurrency — a listener task and a shutdown-event task are raced with asyncio.wait, and signal handlers are registered via loop.add_signal_handler for clean SIGTERM/SIGINT handling.

nodes.name: SKILL.md
nodes.summary: VideoDB skill documenting the Python SDK for video ingest, transcript/search, timeline editing, scene detection, aspect-ratio reframing, desktop capture via WebSocket listener, and generative media features.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Readme for the visa document translator skill, covering OCR-based extraction and Claude-powered translation of passports, visas, and immigration forms with output format, Python library requirements, and usage examples.

nodes.name: listen_with_retry
nodes.summary: Core async coroutine that connects to the VideoDB WebSocket, iterates incoming messages, appends events, and implements exponential back-off retry on transient errors while exiting immediately on fatal ones.

nodes.name: main_async
nodes.summary: Top-level async orchestrator that registers SIGTERM/SIGINT handlers, races the listener task against a shutdown event, cancels pending tasks, and logs final state.

nodes.name: parse_args
nodes.summary: Parses CLI arguments for API key, output directory, and events file path, falling back to environment variables and defaults, and ensures required directories exist with correct permissions.

nodes.name: is_fatal_error
nodes.summary: Classifies an exception as fatal (auth errors, permanent close codes) versus transient to determine whether the retry loop should abort or continue.

nodes.name: append_event
nodes.summary: Appends a received WebSocket event to the JSONL events file with ISO timestamp and Unix epoch metadata.

nodes.name: ensure_private_dir
nodes.summary: Creates a directory if it does not exist and sets its permissions to 0o700 to prevent other users from reading sensitive output files.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Synchronous entry point that writes the PID file, runs the async main coroutine, and cleans up the PID file on exit.
